input_roles.main_reference_image: Locks costume silhouette, cut, layers and palette.
input_roles.supporting_reference_images: Supplement fabric, pattern, accessories, wearing state or detail craftsmanship.

must_preserve: decorations, patterns, wear and wearing logic

layout.sheet_intent: production costume proof board for one outfit identity
layout.background: neutral dark gray production board with subtle grid
modules: color palette, fabric/material swatches, trim and accessory chips
front, side three-quarter and back views of the same outfit
layering breakdown, seam/fastener/embroidery/wear callouts and motion fit examples

identity_badge.fallback: preserve a clean top-left badge plate for post-production overlay

critical_requirements: Output must be COSTUME_DESIGN_SHEET, not a fashion poster or model lookbook.
Apply the subject invariant lock before arranging modules.
Same costume identity across all modules: silhouette, material, color hierarchy, closures and decoration cannot drift.
If a body or mannequin is used, keep it neutral and secondary; do not create a new character identity.
Top-left identity badge must show <costume_short_id> or preserve a clean overlay-ready badge plate.
negative_constraints: no complex scene or advertising poster composition
no loss of fabric, cut, decoration or layer structure
no missing top-left identity badge area

prompt_assembly.final_prompt_template: Create one COSTUME_DESIGN_SHEET for <costume_name> (<costume_short_id>) using <desc> and the reference images. Apply subject_invariant_lock, top-left identity badge, front/side/back views, material swatches, layer breakdown and detail callouts.

review_focus: same costume silhouette and layer order
material, color hierarchy and closures remain stable
neutral body or mannequin does not become a new character
top-left short ID badge or overlay-ready badge plate
